method_atom: a sequential stochastic growth process in which units are added one at a time, each drawing an i.i.d. random scalar attribute, and every new unit attaches to the incumbent that currently holds the record (extremal) value of that attribute. This extremal-attachment rule builds clusters around successive record-holders, with the asymptotic fraction of dominant elements fixed by a universal constant (the Golomb-Dickman constant ~0.6243) tied to record statistics; the process is temporally self-similar at late times and maps onto the combinatorics of cycles of random permutations, randomly broken intervals, and the Kesten multiplicative variable.
object_atom: a growing network whose nodes each carry an intrinsic quality, where each incoming node links to the preexisting node of best quality, forming groups around top-quality 'record' nodes and singling out 'leaders' as the highest-degree nodes.
brief_reason: the record/extremal-driven attachment dynamics and its universal record-statistics and random-permutation combinatorics carry across fields, while the quality-endowed growing network with its leader/group structure is the domain-specific object.
